steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a transition word question. The first sentence states a general principle (potters weigh look and intended use when choosing clay). The second sentence introduces earthenware as a specific instance of that principle. The transition must signal that a concrete example is following.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "In other words,": signals a restatement or paraphrase of the same idea, but the second sentence adds a new specific example rather than restating. ✗
  • "Regardless,": signals a concession or contradiction, implying the second sentence disagrees with the first; both sentences actually support the same idea. ✗
  • "In conclusion,": signals a summary or closing thought, but the passage is not wrapping up — it is illustrating the principle just stated. ✗
  • "For example,": signals that what follows is a specific instance supporting the previous claim — exactly the role earthenware plays here. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
